sourceValidation.title: Incoming JSON rules
sourceValidation.subtitle: Define what the partner payload must satisfy before transformation, without writing code.

error.required: {{path}} is required but missing or empty.

studio.canonicalSubtitle: Declare how data must appear inside your platform: field keys, types, and whether each is required.

canonical.needField: Add at least one canonical field before continuing.
canonical.keyInvalid: Use letters, numbers, underscores, or dots (e.g. order.id).

studio.rulesSubtitle: Wire source paths into canonical targets; choose text, number, date, condition, and array operations from cards. Users never see code; the system turns each choice into an executable rule and validates it.
studio.ruleInspectorEmpty: Select a table row to adjust visual transform options or copy its summary.

ruleContext.sourceHelp: This is the incoming payload field the operation will read.
ruleContext.livePreviewHint: Result updates as you change the rule — no test run needed.
ruleContext.livePreviewEmpty: Choose a source field to see a preview.
ruleContext.livePreviewAdvanced: Advanced rules require a test run to preview.

studio.visualDisabledHint: This rule came from an older technical mode. Clear the technical rule to return to the visual wizard.
studio.advancedHint: Technical rules only appear for imported legacy mappings; the normal flow is the visual wizard.
studio.advancedPlaceholder: The technical rule field is not shown in the normal user flow.
studio.summaryHint: Short description anyone can approve — nothing technical is shown below.
studio.ruleInspectError: This rule failed against the latest test run. Adjust the visual options or sample payloads, then choose Test mapping.

enum.title: Value dictionary
enum.subtitle: Connect incoming partner values to your canonical vocabulary.
enum.unmatchedHint: Unmatched values are left as the source value for now; add another row when needed.

defaultValue.valueLabel: Value to write when source is empty

array.firstHint: Takes the first row from the selected source list.
array.lastHint: Takes the last row from the selected source list.
array.positionHint: Users enter 1 for the first item; the system translates that into the correct list position.
array.countHint: Writes the number of items in the selected list.

math.sumHint: Adds the values in the selected number list.
math.minHint: Selects the smallest value in the selected number list.
math.maxHint: Selects the largest value in the selected number list.

engineProof.title: Engine rule is prepared automatically
engineProof.text: These choices are translated into an executable transformation rule and verified in the test step.

transformSetup.direct: The selected source field is copied into the target field unchanged. No extra settings are needed.
transformSetup.number_coerce: Turns the source value into a number even when it arrives as text; empty values become null.
transformSetup.string_uppercase: Converts the selected text to uppercase.
transformSetup.string_lowercase: Converts the selected text to lowercase.
transformSetup.string_trim: Removes extra spaces from the beginning and end of the text.
transformSetup.noParams: No extra fields are needed for this operation; the choice is verified in the test step.

paths.title: Field selection
paths.text: Choose values from the source tree, including nested fields and single targets.

arrays.title: Arrays
arrays.text: First, last, item by position, filter, count, and join.

strings.title: Text
strings.text: Uppercase, lowercase, trim, replace, slice, and template.

numbers.title: Numbers
numbers.text: Coerce to number, sum, average, minimum, and maximum.

dates.title: Dates
dates.text: Pick input and output formats to reshape dates.

logic.title: Conditions
logic.text: Build if/else rules for business logic.

defaults.title: Defaults
defaults.text: Fill safe values when source data is empty.

validation.title: Validation
validation.text: Preview as you work and run schema gates in the final step.

cheat.title: Mapping tips
cheat.pathsText:
customer.order.reference   — drill into nested JSON
addresses[1].street        — arrays use numeric indexes
Empty paths are ignored safely when you rely on defaults.
cheat.stringsText:
Use the uppercase, lowercase, trim, replace or substring visuals.
Templates let you mix fixed text with {{dynamic.field.paths}} placeholders.
cheat.arraysText:
"Join array" merges values with a delimiter you choose.
"Array element" targets a zero-based slot when only one row matters.
cheat.logicText:
Conditional and default visuals cover most business rules locally.
Rerun Test mapping whenever payloads change.

summary.advanced: Runs an imported technical rule on the whole payload and captures the outcome in “{{target}}”.
summary.direct: Copies the value stored at “{{path}}” into canonical field “{{target}}”.
summary.dateFormat: Treats “{{path}}” as {{inFmt}}, reformats as {{outFmt}}, and publishes to “{{target}}”.
summary.enumMap: Translates partner codes coming from “{{path}}” into your vocabulary on “{{target}}”.
summary.numberCoerce: Parses numeric text at “{{path}}” and stores the number inside “{{target}}”.
summary.defaultValue: Uses live data from “{{path}}” whenever it exists; otherwise writes “{{fallback}}” to “{{target}}”.
summary.combine: Joins values from “{{path}}” plus “{{path2}}”, separated by “{{sep}}”, into “{{target}}”.
summary.stringUppercase: Stores an uppercased version of “{{path}}” inside “{{target}}”.
summary.stringLowercase: Stores a lowercased version of “{{path}}” inside “{{target}}”.
summary.stringTrim: Removes stray spacing from “{{path}}” before writing “{{target}}”.
summary.stringSubstring: Slices “{{path}}” beginning at character index {{start}}{{lenPhrase}}, then assigns to “{{target}}”.
summary.substrToEndPhrase: , through the remainder of the text
summary.stringReplace: Replaces every “{{from}}” with “{{to}}” in “{{path}}” for field “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayJoin: Flattens the array at “{{path}}”, separated by “{{delim}}”, straight into “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayFirst: Takes the first item from “{{path}}” and maps it onto “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayLast: Takes the last item from “{{path}}” and maps it onto “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayElement: Takes item {{index}} from “{{path}}” and maps it onto “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayCount: Counts the items in “{{path}}” and writes the number to “{{target}}”.
summary.arrayFilterEquals: Keeps rows in “{{path}}” where “{{itemPath}}” equals “{{value}}”, then maps the filtered list to “{{target}}”.
summary.mathSum: Adds the number list at “{{path}}” and writes the result to “{{target}}”.
summary.mathAverage: Averages the number list at “{{path}}” and writes the result to “{{target}}”.
summary.mathMin: Writes the smallest number from “{{path}}” to “{{target}}”.
summary.mathMax: Writes the largest number from “{{path}}” to “{{target}}”.
summary.conditional: If “{{path}}” matches “{{when}}”, emits “{{thenVal}}”; otherwise emits “{{elseVal}}”, both heading to “{{target}}”.
summary.template: Builds “{{target}}” from the reusable template authored on the Visual tab.

studio.altFixtureSubtitle: Optional second JSON using the same rules — useful for regression-style checks without leaving the wizard.
studio.validateSubtitle: Executes transforms locally against the sample JSON and checks required canonical fields (demo-only, no API).
studio.validateSubtitleHybrid: Runs mappings in the browser, checks required fields, and verifies compile/eval parity in the same engine used by ingestion.

test.successToast: Test passed — output matches required fields.
test.failedToast: Test failed — review validation messages and rules.
test.running: Running mapping rules and schema checks...

studio.publishSuccess: Demo publish recorded. In production this version would be promoted and traffic would route to the new mapping.

publish.blocked: Fix validation errors and run a successful test before publishing.

studio.outputSubtitle: Preview of the canonical payload after applying your rules.

validation.allPresent: All required canonical fields are present.
validation.ruleEvalFailed: The rule driving “{{field}}” could not complete. Inspect that column in the inspector and rerun Test mapping.
validation.transformerUnreachable: The transformation engine check could not be reached. Ensure the service is running and CORS allows this origin.
validation.serverBrowserMismatch: The production engine result differs from browser output for canonical field “{{field}}”. Check library versions—server result is authoritative in production pipelines.

transformHelp.combine: Join two fields into one output
transformHelp.string_substring: Take a specific part of text
transformHelp.array_join: Flatten a list with a delimiter
transformHelp.array_count: Write the number of list items
transformHelp.array_filter_equals: Keep rows where an item field equals a value
transformHelp.conditional_value: Write one value if matched, another if not

schema.unsupportedRoot: Unsupported schema: root must be type "object".
schema.canonicalFailedSummary: Canonical JSON Schema validation failed (see details below).

unfinished.title: Unfinished work
unfinished.subtitle: Clear required fields, rule errors, and coverage gaps before publish.
unfinished.readyText: All required target fields are connected to mapping rules. You can run fixture and schema gates in the final test step.
unfinished.needsWorkText: {{count}} required target field(s) are still unmapped. Each row opens the exact target field in the workbench.
unfinished.coverageSubtitle: A compact health summary that gives confidence in a sales demo.
unfinished.blockedDetail: Map the required fields in Unfinished work before continuing to tests.

fixture.title: Fixture batch runner
fixture.subtitle: Run the current mapping rules against multiple input/expected pairs (kept separate from the single alternate JSON box above).
fixture.name: Name
fixture.configNoFixtures: No fixtures array found in this config file.
fixture.configImported: {{count}} fixture row(s) imported with inline payloads.
fixture.configPathsImported: {{count}} fixture row(s) imported from config paths. Browser security cannot read local files by path; drop each input/expected JSON onto the row.

expression.title: Generated transformation rule
expression.subtitle: Executable rule preview automatically prepared from the visual wizard choices.
